review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9:
Diversified Portfolio: JSW Group has a diversified portfolio across multiple sectors, which helps mitigate risks associated with economic fluctuations in any single industry.

Industry Leader: In the steel sector, JSW Steel is one of the leading producers in India and ranks among the top steel companies globally. Their strong market position reflects their expertise and capabilities in the industry.

Innovation and Technology: JSW Group emphasizes innovation and technology adoption across its businesses, enabling them to stay competitive and enhance operational efficiency.

Commitment to Sustainability: JSW Group is committed to sustainable practices and has taken initiatives to reduce its environmental footprint, such as investing in renewable energy and implementing eco-friendly technologies.

Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR): The company actively engages in CSR activities, focusing on areas like education, healthcare, environment, and community development, contributing to the well-being of society.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
Dependency on Economic Conditions: Like many conglomerates, JSW Group's performance is susceptible to economic conditions. Economic downturns can affect demand for their products and impact their financial results.

Regulatory Challenges: Operating in highly regulated sectors like steel and energy can subject JSW Group to regulatory challenges, including environmental regulations, government policies, and compliance requirements.

Market Volatility: Fluctuations in commodity prices, currency exchange rates, and other market factors can impact the profitability of JSW Group's businesses, leading to volatility in financial performance.

Competition: JSW Group faces competition from both domestic and international players across its various business segments. Competing in crowded markets requires continuous innovation and strategic positioning to maintain market share.

Infrastructure Constraints: Infrastructure constraints such as transportation, logistics, and energy availability can pose challenges to JSW Group's operations and expansion plans, especially in regions with inadequate infrastructure development.

review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9:
Growth Opportunities and Learning: JSW Steel is one of India’s largest and most dynamic steel companies, offering employees exposure to cutting-edge technologies and large-scale industrial projects. It provides ample opportunities for professional development, skill enhancement, and career progression.

Strong Organizational Culture: JSW Steel promotes a culture of innovation, sustainability, and employee engagement. The company values its workforce and emphasizes work-life balance, health, and safety, making it a conducive environment for long-term career growth.

review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2: The job started off with me getting placed from my college in this company. This company looks all fancy from the outside but on the inside it is filled with nuisance. People don’t know what work they have to do, I joined in the investor relations team. This was supposed to be an internship converted into a PPO, but that never happened. They extended my internship and showed full intent that it would be a full time job but the thing was there was no job that was created in the first place. They had to create one for me which was on top management and we all know that it rarely ever happens. I worked there for three months and it was all in vain. I don’t recommend working here. My college was over and I had not been placed yet as a fresher this is the worst thing that could happen. Post this I discovered that JSW Steel doesn’t retain 90% of the interns as the company makes them do so much work that they run away.

review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9: Great Opportunity. Being a private company, manpower is tight and they will make you highly responsible. This is tough on one side, but you can really learn and grow if you utilize that opportunity. Their top notch technology in steel manufacturing (cold rolling mill) is built on par with Japan, and is the best-in-segment practice in Indian steel industry. Their packages are not liberal, but if you can show real progress, you will be highly rewarded. It depends on your boss review as well. This is what I saw in my one year tenure at JSW-Bellary, Karnataka.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2: Mundane Life. You feel like you are locked in a small campus, and not much choice for social or party-going people. You be in their campus forever until you find a next opportunity. You will work for 6 days a week, more than official 8 hours per day, with no incentives for extra time or work. Your career escalation will be very slow: they have many position within junior and middle management. Very few people climb to senior positions.

review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9: There are a handful of GOOD HELPFUL PEOPLE still remaining in the company and they are its only hope!
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
MY GOD! Where do I even begin! I served this company in its Bellary unit in Electrical Maintenance. 

- Human Resource Dept: The WORST HR TEAM EVER! Although it is headed by one of the best VP in the company, the mid level management is just GOD AWFUL to deal with! Its like a typical Government Office where they delay every small minuscule work and refuse to agree to whatever you say! They treat you like you are a peasant and they are your KINGS! They are the biggest reason to hold the company back! 

By the way, your mentioned salary will nowhere be even close to what you get in hand! 

- Township: Most of them have mentioned it has a GREAT TOWNSHIP and all that but trust me, the colours start fading just after a month or so! We called it a sweet prison. Imagine being a contestant of BIG BOSS for the rest of your life! There are a total of 5-6 eateries with a limited menu which you'll be bored within 6 months max. The township does have certain facilities like the lake and sports club which are worth mentioning BUT if you join a production unit ( SMS, BF, Mills etc) or if you're put in shift, kindly forget about it. In fact you may also bid goodbye to your health and your routine if you are into SHI(F)T.  

- Work Culture: If you hail from a college that has taught you soft skills, corporate culture or anything even vaguely related to it, just toss it right out of the window! Managers speak to you like you're their laborer and as if you owe something to them. People here only work to impress their bosses and everyone changes colour by the beginning of February getting ready for an appraisal. They will stoop to any level, use every word in the Indian Slang Dictionary and speak to you like they're some MAFIA.

- Working Condition: If you work onsite, you're doomed for life! Get ready to be yelled at in the middle of the night coz some machine stopped working! PRODUCTION is the only word in the management dictionary. My roommate was into project and I was't even sure if he existed or was absconding till his project was complete. Very risky working conditions and you may also have to risk your life at times just to avoid a minute's delay!

I can go on and on about this ridiculous work environment but I have a much better job now which I love!

All in all, this is a company meant for people who believe that this is the best they could achieve and they are worth this torture because their IQ is limited to this kind of demeaning work environment. A sincere request to any new joiners to leave all your ethical, moral, corporate values right back at your home. And yes! Enjoy the first 3 months of training coz that's the only thing you'll be getting from them.

GOD SAVE YOU ALL!

This review is pertaining to Dolvi plant Location
 
General View
  
-  Senior Professionals tend to keep all the employee related information to themselves. They don't even share it with you even if you are working with them on some assignment.

- Some newly hired professionals in the junior management turned out to be more competent than those in the middle management and hence this created a sense of insecurity among the two senior professionals and hence these people resorted to all sorts of politics and what not, to make sure the impression of the junior managers is spoiled. 

- There are no systems and processes in the Dolvi Plant. Therefore insane amounts of paper is wasted taking print outs ( this is so because, even if you are taking a print of excel sheet that is in the required format but the Sr. Vp HR has somehow forgotten the mutually decided format , he wold then ask you to change it! )

- 6 days working

- No platform to address employee grievances

- Employee policies are not at all employee friendly.

- GPTW Survey engagement score for Dolvi plant is the highest in the JSW Group . Yet as a junior manager, off the countless employees I interacted with I never came across an employee who was genuinely happy working 
      

Growth and Development

- There is absolutely no scope of learning and self development ( No mentorship or coaching is provided), that is because junior management is only involved in administrative tasks and are not given the freedom to express their opinions and ideas. 

- People in junior management are not even nominated for any type of training, even though they feel that they require it.

- You might become a victim to continuous Harassment


Recruitment

- Recruitment based on physical appearance
 
Systems and processes

- We are living in an age where the whole world is going digital and its kind of sad to see that this plant still lacks some basic HR systems in place. 

 - Some key projects are being assigned to the consultants but there is lack of coordination between the internal team and the consultant team so the outcome of the projects is far from the ground reality.
